title: Rich, Indulgent Greek Porcini Avgolemono
description: A spring-inspired Greek dish featuring porcini mushrooms, a rich avgolemono sauce, and a delicate balance of flavors, all on a bed of springy, tender rice.

ingredients.name: Grated Parmesan Cheese
ingredients.notes: Shojin-friendly option: omit or substitute with a plant-based alternative.

instructions: In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the sliced porcini mushrooms and cook until golden brown, about 8 minutes, stirring occasionally.

In a large saucepan, combine the chicken broth, rice, salt, and black pepper. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender, about 18-20 minutes.

In a separate saucepan, whisk together the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, and egg yolks. Cook over medium heat, whisking constantly, until the sauce thickens and coats the back of a spoon, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir the cooked porcini mushrooms into the yogurt sauce and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until heated through.
Transfer the rice mixture to a baking dish and top with the yogurt porcini sauce, followed by the grated Parmesan cheese.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the flavors have melded together.
Remove from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley and dill. Serve immediately.
Garnish with additional Parmesan cheese, if desired.
Serve in a slate-tile vessel for an added touch of elegance.
